notes: Super 8 is a retro polysynth running inside Reaktor 6, inspired by the lush 70s and 80s analog polyphonic synthesizers such as the Sequential Circuits Prophet-5 and Roland Jupiter-8. It supports up to 8-voice polyphony with unison stacking and detune for thick, wide pads and leads. The filter is warm and musical with a vintage character, and the onboard chorus and ensemble effect is essential to its sound — the chorus is often the difference between a thin patch and a fully realised analog-style pad. CC 74 (Brightness) maps to Filter Cutoff and is the most important real-time tonal control. CC 1 (Modulation) modulates the filter cutoff or vibrato depth depending on the preset, providing the classic mod wheel vibrato that is standard on poly synths of this era. Super 8 runs as a Reaktor ensemble, so Quick Control assignments may vary per preset, but the layout below reflects the default assignments.

Volume.description: Master output level of the Reaktor ensemble

Expression.description: Dynamic expression / output level — useful for swells on pad patches

Modulation.description: Vibrato depth or filter cutoff modulation — classic mod wheel vibrato for lead lines

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — holds notes; effective for pad textures and long chord progressions

Release Time.description: Envelope release time — longer releases for lush pad tails

Attack Time.description: Envelope attack time — slow attacks create pad-like swells from percussive presets

Brightness.description: Filter Cutoff — primary tonal control; rolls off the multi-mode analog-style filter

Reverb Send.description: Reverb wet level — add space to the naturally dry analog-style sound

Quick Control 1.description: Filter Cutoff — main cutoff frequency of the analog-style filter; key expressive control

Quick Control 2.description: Filter Resonance — adds resonant peak; at high values creates filter self-oscillation

Quick Control 3.description: Attack — envelope attack time for both VCA and VCF simultaneously

Quick Control 4.description: Release — envelope release time; extend for long, lush pad tails

Quick Control 5.description: Chorus Rate — speed of the onboard chorus/ensemble effect; slower for shimmer, faster for vibrato-like effect

Quick Control 6.description: Chorus Depth — intensity of the chorus modulation; essential for the characteristic wide poly-synth sound

Quick Control 7.description: Reverb — onboard reverb send level within the ensemble

Quick Control 8.description: Unison Detune — amount of detuning between stacked unison voices for width and thickness
